old_claim.note: Advanced Stone Tools is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: At the Ngwenya anchor, choppers, picks, and hammers made of dolerite were identified as mining tools, making stone-tool capability the practical tool background for testing and working exposed mineral sources.

source_shape.source_locator: Justification of Outstanding Universal Value: ancient mining tools at the site were choppers, picks, and hammers made of dolerite and were identified as mining tools.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: UNESCO identifies stone choppers, picks, and hammers as the tool assemblage for early Ngwenya mineral extraction.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The replacement narrows the prerequisite from broad Acheulean advanced stone tools to the actual stone-tool capability documented at the mineral-source anchor.

ontology_before: Prospecting depended on Advanced Stone Tools, laundering a broad Acheulean bifacial tradition into mineral-source recognition.
ontology_after: Prospecting is scoped to recognizing and testing exposed mineral sources; stone_tool_making supplies the documented tool background for the Ngwenya anchor.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: prospecting now depends on stone_tool_making and no longer directly depends on advanced_stone_tools.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to modern geological prospecting, where stone tools are irrelevant.
A source showed the first scoped mineral-source recognition required Acheulean bifaces specifically rather than basic stone tools.
The Ngwenya anchor were moved out to a separate mining-only node and prospecting were left as a purely observational skill.
short_reason: The source-backed tool claim is basic stone mining tools, not the whole advanced-stone-tool tradition.
